creator: kristinacors
caption: Which spot are YOU running to first?  #luxuryhotels #thingstodoincharleston #hiddengemsincharleston  #outerbanks #charlestonsouthcarolina

transcription.text: Three places in Charleston I keep coming back to again and again. First up is this treehouse on Daniel Island. Something about this place makes my inner child so happy. I don't know about you, but when I was little, I always wanted to build a treehouse. And I don't know if it's a combination of the moss and it being on the water, but being here just makes me feel like I'm on the Netflix show The Outer Banks. By the way, the location of this treehouse is Smith Street. Second is Camellias at Hotel Bennett. I don't think I'll ever get over this jaw-dropping crystal chandelier. It would honestly be my dream to buy this chandelier and install it in my future home. You'll want to come for afternoon tea, upscale bites, and of course, champagne. Camellias is definitely too iconic to skip. Okay, third is this neighborhood. If you love the show The Outer Banks, you'll really, really love this neighborhood. I don't know if it's just me, but I just feel like Charleston is super, super underrated. Like, the fact that these homes have such lush greenery surrounding them just blows my mind. Anyway, the name of this neighborhood is ION. Follow for more things to do in Charleston and around the world.
